converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Alison Lurie papers
Lurie, Alison.
Kaufman, Sue, 1926-1977.
Garnett, David, 1892-1981.
Gorey, Edward, 1925-2000.
Isherwood, Christopher, 1904-1986.
Manning, Olivia.
Mizener, Arthur.
Powell, Anthony, 1905-2000.
Powell, Violet, 1912-2002.
Roth, Philip.
Stafford, Jean, 1915-1979.
Wilson, Edmund, 1895-1972.
Bellow, Saul.
Creeley, Robert, 1926-2005.
Lurie, Alison. (Title of work: Love and friendship..)
Lurie, Alison. (Title of work: Imaginary friends..)
Lurie, Alison. (Title of work: Real people..)
Lurie, Alison. (Title of work: Foreign affairs..)
Lurie, Alison. (Title of work: War between the Tates..)
Lurie, Alison. (Title of work: Truth about Lorin Jones..)
Lurie, Alison. (Title of work: Women and ghosts..)
Lurie, Alison. (Title of work: Last resort..)
Lurie, Alison. (Title of work: Truth and consequences..)
Lurie, Alison. (Title of work: Familiar spirits..)
McClatchy, Sandy.
14-12-2572
8 cubic feet. 8 cubic feet.
1960-2013 Notes, drafts, proofs, manuscript, correspondence. Notes, drafts, and printer's proofs of books by Lurie including Love and Friendship, Imaginary Friends, Real People, Foreign Affairs, The War Between the Tates, The Truth About Lorin Jones, Women and Ghosts, and The Last Resort; manuscript of a short story; and correspondence. Correspondents include Saul Bellow, Robert Creeley, Sue Kaufman, David Garnett, Edward Gorey, Christopher Isherwood, Olivia Manning, Arthur Mizener, Anthony and Violet Powell, Philip Roth, Jean Stafford, and Edmund Wilson. Also, a draft of the first chapter of Real People, annotated by Philip Roth. Collection material in English 2021081
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Julia Parker, Janette Park
EAD encoding:
Mireille Lee, July 2000
Date modified:
Fredrika Loew, March 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Alison Lurie was born in 1926 and is the author of Love and Friendship (1962), Imaginary Friends (1967), The War Between the Tates, Real People (1969), Only Children (1979), Language of Clothes (1981), and other novels. Professor emerita of English at Cornell University.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Notes, drafts, and printer's proofs of books by Lurie including Love and Friendship, Imaginary Friends, Real People, Foreign Affairs, The War Between the Tates, The Truth About Lorin Jones, Women and Ghosts, and The Last Resort; manuscript of a short story; and correspondence. Correspondents include Saul Bellow, Robert Creeley, Sue Kaufman, David Garnett, Edward Gorey, Christopher Isherwood, Olivia Manning, Arthur Mizener, Anthony and Violet Powell, Philip Roth, Jean Stafford, and Edmund Wilson. Also, a draft of the first chapter of Real People, annotated by Philip Roth.
SERIES LIST
Real People
Box 1
Love and Friendship
Box 1
Ro of Varna
Box 2
Imaginary Friends
Box 2
V. R. Lang - A Memoir
Box 2
Foreign Affairs
Boxes 3, 4
The Truth About Lorin Jones
Boxes 4-6, 8
The War Between the Tates
Box 4
Counting Sheep
Box 4
Women and Ghosts
Box 7
The Last Resort
Boxes 9, 10, 11
The Nowhere City
Box 11
Access Restrictions:
Access to Box 19 is restricted until 2093
Cite As:
Alison Lurie Papers, #14-12-2572. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Related Materials
Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.library.cornell.edu/handle/1813/40577
Authors, American. Women authors.
